questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
For a gaseous phase first order reaction
$4 \mathrm{~A}(\mathrm{~g}) \xrightarrow{\mathrm{K}} \mathrm{B}(\mathrm{g})+6 \mathrm{C}(\mathrm{g})$ the rate constant is given by
$\mathrm{K}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{Pt}} \ln \left(\frac{\mathrm{QP}_0}{R \mathrm{P}_0-\mathrm{SP}_{\mathrm{t}}}\right)$ when expressed in simplest whole numbers, where $\mathrm{P}_0$ is the initial pressure of ' A ' and $P_t$ is the total pressure at any time ' $t$ '. Report the value of $(R+S)-(P+Q)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & 4 A \quad \rightarrow B+6 C \\ & P_0-4 x \\ & P_t=P_0+3 x \Rightarrow x=\left(\frac{P_t-P_0}{3}\right) \\ & \therefore 4 K t=\ln \left(\frac{P_0}{P_0-4 x}\right) \\ & =\ln \frac{P_0}{P_0-\frac{4\left(P_t-P_0\right)}{3}} \\ & =\ln \left(\frac{3 P_0}{7 P_0-4 P_t}\right) \\ & \therefore \mathrm{K}=\frac{1}{4 t} \ln \left(\frac{3 P_0}{7 P_0-4 P_t}\right) \\ & \therefore P=4, Q=3, Q=7, S=4 \\ & \therefore(R+S)-(P+Q)=11-7=4\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: A vessel at 100 K contains $\mathrm{CO}_2$ with a pressure of 0.5 atm . Some of the $\mathrm{CO}_2$ is converted into CO on addition of graphite. Calculate the value of $K_p$ if the total pressure at equilibrium is 0.8 atm .

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
500 ml of $0.150 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{AgNO}_3$ solution is mixed with 500 ml of $1.09 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ solution and the reaction is allowed to reach equilibrium at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$.
$\mathrm{Ag}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Fe}^{3+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{~s})$

For 25 ml of the equilibrium solution, 30 ml of $0.0833 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{KMnO}_4$ were required for oxidation. Calculate the approximate equilibrium constant for the reaction at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$.
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{Ag}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}(\mathrm{aq})$

\begin{array}{ll}No. of mmoles initially & 500 \times 0.15 & 500 \times 1.09 & 0 & 0\\ & =75 & =545 \\mmoles at equilib & 75-x & 545-x & x & x\end{array}
On titration of reaction mixture with $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$, only $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ reacts with it.
$\therefore$ Equivalents of $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ in $25 \mathrm{ml}=$ Equivalents of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$
$\frac{(545-x)}{1000} \times 25 \times 10^{-3} \times 1=30 \times 10^{-3} \times 0.0833 \times 5$
$\begin{aligned} & \quad \therefore K_c=\frac{\frac{(545-x) \times 25}{1000}}{\left[\mathrm{Ag}^{3+}\right]\left[\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}\right]}=\frac{4.5 ; 545-x}{\frac{(75-45)}{1000} \times \frac{(545-45)}{1000}}=500 ; x=45 \\ & 30 \times 500\end{aligned} 3.0$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
For the reaction:
$\mathrm{SnO}_2(\mathrm{~s})+2 \mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{Sn}(\mathrm{~s})$
Calculate $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{P}}$ at 900 K , where the equilibrium steam-hydrogen mixture was $45 \% \mathrm{H}_2$ by volume.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A 100 ml flask containing $\mathrm{O}_2$ at 1.2 atm and 300 K is connected to a 250 ml flask containing $\mathrm{NO}(\mathrm{g})$ at 0.6 atm and 300 K , by means of a narrow tube of negligible volume, when they combine quantitatively to form $\mathrm{NO}_2$. Finally $\mathrm{NO}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ dimerized, partially into $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_4(\mathrm{~g})$ and pressure inside the flask was found to be 0.41 atm at the same temperature. Find $\%$ dimerization of $\mathrm{NO}_2$ as
$2 \mathrm{NO}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_4(\mathrm{~g})$ (mark the answer in %)
questions.solution:
$2 \mathrm{NO}+\mathrm{O}_2 \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{NO}_2 \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{O}_4$
$\begin{array}{lll}\text { Initial } & 0.4285 & 0.3428\end{array}$
$\begin{array}{llll}\text { partial press. } 0 & 0.12855 & 0.4285\end{array}$
$0.4285-P \quad \frac{P}{2}$
Total pressure $x$ at the end
$\begin{aligned}
& P=0.55704-\frac{P}{2}=0.41 \\
& \Rightarrow P=0.2941 \\
& \% \text { dimerization }=\frac{0.2941}{0.4285} \times 100 \\
& =68.63 \%
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
